channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: “I Survived On Garri, Sold Tomatoes, Fish” – Super Eagles’ Moses Simon Shares Struggles
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Super Eagles winger Moses Simon has opened up on the hardships he endured before becoming a professional footballer. He revealed that he survived on garri and water for months while chasing his dream. Simon, who currently plays for French side Paris FC, made the disclosure in an interview with Urban

item.description:

item.title: Three Mexicans, seven others remanded over N480bn meth lab
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:

item.title: The United States of MAGAs, dreamers and survivors, By Owei Lakemfa
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The Mount Rushmore National Memorial, South Dakota, is a 1,278-acre or 517-hectare tract seized from the Sioux Nation of India. Into its mountain face are sculpted the 18-metre or 60-foot-high figures of four former United States (US) Presidents. They feature founding President George Washington, ma

item.description: Police have restricted movement from 12 midnight to 6 pm across Edo state as a result of today’s local government area election. The post Police restrict movement over Edo LG election appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.description: The minister explained that airport regulations require drivers to remain behind the wheel while using the drop-off area and that unattended vehicles are liable to enforcement action because of the security risks they pose.

item.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way," the company said in a blog post. "We've heard the feedback that this feature missed the mark, so it's no longer available."

item.title: Meta turns off the Instagram feature that let users make AI deepfakes of public accounts
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Following significant backlash, Meta is turning off the feature it announced this week that let users generate AI images based on content from public Instagram accounts just by tagging them. The feature, as originally set up, meant that content from any public Instagram account could be used in AI c

item.title: Forced to wait his turn, Marine Le Pen's deputy Bardella returns to the shadows
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Thirty-year-old Jordan Bardella had been groomed for success as France's next hard-right leader. He will now have to wait longer.

item.title: MGM Resorts Engaged in Deal Talks With Diller, WSJ Says
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: MGM Resorts International is engaged in deal talks with Barry Diller’s People Inc. after the entertainment mogul offered to acquire the part of the casino company he doesn’t already own, the Wall Street Journal reported.

item.title: Apple files lawsuit accusing ChatGPT maker OpenAI of stealing trade secrets
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The complaint alleges a coordinated effort by two former Apple employees and OpenAI to steal confidential information.

item.title: ECB face no action over Stokes retirement video
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: No action will be taken by the International Cricket Council (ICC) over Ben Stokes' retirement video.

item.title: Madugu Names Strong Super Falcons Squad For WAFCON Title Defence
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Head Coach Justine Madugu has unveiled a 25-player Super Falcons squad led by captain Rasheedat Ajibade and goalkeeper Chiamaka Nnadozie as Nigeria prepares to defend its Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON) title at the 2026 tournament in Morocco. The 14th edition of the competition will take pla

item.title: Apple sues OpenAI, its employees claiming theft of trade secrets
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Apple said in a Friday lawsuit that OpenAI’s nascent hardware business is “rotten to its core.”

item.title: S&P Global: FIFA to Offer Short-Term Economic Boost
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Nora Wittstruck, Chief Analytical Officer for Governments at S&P Global, joined Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on Bloomberg's 'The Close' to provide insights on FIFA's financial position during the 2026 World Cup. She highlights how FIFA has emerged financially stronger despite facing numero

item.title: Mutfwang Commissions Potato Value Chain Projects, Distributes Agricultural Equipment
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Plateau State Governor, Barr. Caleb Manasseh Mutfwang on Friday commissioned the Potato Value Chain Project, a state-of-the-art Tissue Culture Laboratory and the distribution of modern agricultural equipment in Mangu Local Government Area. ‎ ‎The initiative is a landmark investment that will transfo

item.title: “Nigeria Has Never Experienced Advancements We’re Seeing Under Tinubu” — Ooni of Ife
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The Ooni of Ife, Oba Adeyeye Enitan Ogunwusi, Ojaja II, has hailed President Bola Tinubu’s administration, saying Nigeria is witnessing unprecedented progress under his leadership. Speaking at the inauguration of the PBAT Door-to-Door Movement in Abuja on Thursday, the monarch commended Tinubu’s eco

item.title: Airtel Targets 5,000 Connected Schools As Poor Internet Access Threatens Learning
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Despite growing digital transformation across Africa, millions of schoolchildren, particularly in rural and underserved communities, remain cut off from quality online learning because of limited internet access and inadequate digital infrastructure. Against this backdrop, Airtel Africa has pledged

item.title: Get Your PVCs Now To Determine Nigeria’s Future, NDC Urges Members, Nigerians
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), has called on all members of the party, supporters and every patriotic Nigerian to take full advantage of the ongoing Continuous Voter’s Registration(CVR) exercise and obtain their Permanent Voter Card (PVC). NDC National Publicity Secretary, Osa Director

item.title: Colombia’s Jaminton Campaz receives death threats after World Cup exit
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Campaz was threatened after failing to score in the last-16 match against Switzerland, which Colombia lost on penalties.
